title: Air-fryer chicken drumsticks
subtitle: Crisp skin, tender meat, 25 minutes
excerpt: Chicken drumsticks coated in a smoked paprika and garlic dry rub, cooked in the air fryer at 200°C for 25 minutes. The skin crisps properly under the circulating heat without any oil beyond a light coating on the chicken. Pull at 80°C internal for moist, falling-from-the-bone meat.
sourceNotes: Chicken drumsticks are one of the best cuts for the air fryer because the bone acts as a heat sink that keeps the internal temperature from spiking too fast, giving the skin time to render and crisp before the inside overcooks. This method applies a simple dry rub and produces results in under 30 minutes.

recipe.makeAheadNotes: Season and refrigerate up to 24 hours ahead.

content.text: Score the skin two or three times before seasoning. A knife cut through to the bone lets the dry rub penetrate the meat rather than sitting only on the surface, and it lets the fat under the skin render out during cooking. Without scoring, the skin can balloon away from the meat and the result is pale and uneven.

Preheat the air fryer to 200°C for 3 minutes. Score each {{chicken-drumsticks}} 2 to 3 times through the skin to the bone. Toss with the {{vegetable-oil}}, {{smoked-paprika}}, {{garlic-powder}}, {{onion-powder}}, {{sea-salt-fine}}, and {{black-pepper}} until well coated. Arrange in the basket in a single layer, skin-side up where possible. Cook at 200°C for 25 minutes, turning once at the 12-minute mark, until the skin is deeply golden and the internal temperature reads 80°C. Rest for 3 minutes.

items.symptom: Skin still pale and soft after 25 minutes
items.cause: Basket overcrowded or chicken not dried before seasoning
items.fix: Cook in batches of no more than 6 drumsticks in a standard basket. Pat dry before oiling and seasoning: moisture prevents the skin from crisping

items.symptom: Skin very dark but centre still not quite done
items.cause: Air fryer runs hot, or drumsticks larger than average
items.fix: Reduce temperature to 180°C after the first 15 minutes and continue for another 10 to 15 minutes. Large drumsticks need more time at a lower temperature to ensure the centre cooks without the outside burning

items.symptom: Meat pulling away from the bone and drying out
items.fix: Check with a thermometer. Drumsticks are done at 80°C internal: the carry-over heat after resting finishes the job

content.text: Air-fryer drumsticks are a weeknight staple for families because they are cheap, quick, and require almost no preparation. Serve with air-fryer chips or rice and a green vegetable. The dry rub scales to any spice profile: swap the smoked paprika and garlic for lemon zest and Italian seasoning for a Mediterranean version, or use Chinese five-spice for an Asian-style glaze.
